about_full: The Central Bureau of Statistics has gone through several stages since its inception â€¢ It was established as a department in the Customs Authority to collect foreign trade statistics 1903. â€¢â€¢ It was transformed into the Ministry of Economic Planning in 1969.
about_full2: The world has recently witnessed a marked increase in quantity and quality in the demand for statistical data and information. The remarkable advances in ict have accelerated the demand for statistical information that is time-given, comprehensive, reliable and flexible. At the national level, the continued development of the development path in the Sudan also requires more accurate, comprehensive and timely statistics to support decision-making, policy-making, progress monitoring and impact and performance assessment. The continued response to these increasing demands, both in terms of quality, requires the Central Bureau of Statistics of the Sudan to find a mechanism that will enable it to continue to develop its work in a sustainable manner, while keeping pace with relevant developments inside and outside the Sudan at the same time by building a genuine partnership with the aim of drawing on experiences and experiences, and joining efforts to promote this vital sector.
about_full3:
In 1903, the Bureau of Statistics (present-day Central Bureau of Statistics) was a section of the Customs Service whose main function was to prepare foreign trade statistics.
By 1932, this census department became what was known as the Economic Commission, which in 1934 became the Department of Commerce and Economy.
In 1953, the Department of Statistics separated from the Department of Commerce and Economy and became an independent, stand-alone department known as the Department of Statistics and belonged to the Ministry of Social Affairs at that time, and in November 1958 the Statistics Department became subordinate to the Prime Minister's Office.
In 1969, the Ministry of Economic Planning was established and a decision was issued to subordinate the Statistics Department to this ministry.
On November 6, 1995, the Cabinet's decision to establish the Central Bureau of Statistics and its affiliation with the Minister of Finance and National Economy was issued.
about_stat: The Central Bureau of Statistics is the body concerned with statistics and is the only official authority for the implementation of the statistical work and the technical supervisor and organizer, and has in order to achieve its objectives the following disciplines:
about_stat2: Preparing a national strategy for statistical work in the country in coordination with the relevant public authorities, and following up its implementation after its approval and proposing to update it periodically.
about_stat3: Carry out statistical work in accordance with accepted international standards, including:
about_stat_meth: Determining the methodology of statistical work.
about_statdesgin: Design and implement surveys and preparations.
about_stat7: Data and information in statistical documents.
about01: Collecting data and information covering all aspects of life in Sudan from its various sources, codifying and tabulating, collecting data and information from administrative records in public entities and private enterprises, tabulating, analysing and extracting their indicators.
about02: Preparing statistical evidence and classifications in accordance with international standards, using them and working to update and develop them when needed.
about03: Preparing statistical bulletins and reports for surveys, research, etc.
about04: The formation of a comprehensive system of statistical databases for various statistical fields.
about05: The establishment of a centralized information system in the Central Bureau of Statistics at the national level is automatically linked to all public bodies.
about06: The establishment of a centralized information system in the Central Bureau of Statistics at the national level is automatically linked to all public bodies.
about07: The mission of the agency is not only to provide data at the local level, but goes beyond that to include the statistical contribution in the regional and international environment through the feeding of regional and global databases.
